sgTip: Auron is now permanent. Prioritize Power Break and Armor Break on his grid — they're invaluable from here through the endgame.

subLocations.name: Mi'ihen Highroad South
subLocations.prose: Most people on the Highroad are happy to hand things over — talk to everyone. Maechen on the left side gives the historian's spiel if you want the lore. Aerial enemies like the Zu are best handled by Wakka; use Lulu to blind the Dual Horns. After meeting the Chocobo Knights, drop into the right-side alcove to fight Belgemine for the first time — win with Valefor's Energy Ray and she hands over an Echo Ring. Dual Horns can inflict Darkness and have high physical defence, so magic is more efficient — Lulu's Thunder cuts through most highroad fiends quickly; also use Kimahri's Lancet on a Funguar here to learn Bad Breath.

subLocations.name: Rin's Agency / Newroad
subLocations.prose: Rin introduces himself on the way out and hands over Primer VIII automatically. After defeating the Chocobo Eater, borrow Rin's chocobo — it lets you skip encounters and reach a feather-marked ledge north of the bridges for the Heat Lance, plus Primer IX is on the ground nearby. The Mars Crest is a detour back to Oldroad South, but it's not missable — you can grab it via airship later if you skip it now. Raldo and Ipiria in this stretch are physically tanky, so Water and Thunder magic cuts through them faster than physical attacks; steal Remedies from Floating Death (the flying eye enemies) for a cheap cure to the status effects this stretch inflicts.

items.name: Al Bhed Primer Vol. VIII (free from Rin)

bosses.strategy: Start with Lulu, Tidus, and Wakka — cast Haste on Lulu so she fires off more spells, and use Wakka's Dark Attack to blind it (blinded, its attacks become much less accurate). Substitute Kimahri and Auron in later; Auron's Power Break reduces incoming physical damage. If knocked prone, the Chocobo Eater loses its armored status and non-piercing weapons deal full damage — each attack pushing it backward over 500 HP also shoves it toward the cliff. Push it off the edge for the best result: Rin lets you use a chocobo for free. If it pushes your party off the cliff instead, you land at the bottom of the gorge with no reward and have to climb back up.
